commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1798438fe25b19010de99f43fb471f711a895371)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds read-only Skill Workshop list-time stale detection for create/update proposals, refactors stale marking into a helper, and adds focused service tests plus a standalone proof script.

PR surface: Source +52, Tests +68, Other +152. Total +272 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows listSkillProposals returns persisted manifest status without target-state reconciliation, and the linked issue describes a create proposal whose target skill was manually installed later.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor needs to repair the list/inspect/UI consistency gap and provide final real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not take over while external proof remains required.

target.title: fix(skills): reconcile stale workshop proposals when target skill exists or is missing
target.body:
## Summary

When a pending create proposal's target skill file already exists (e.g. user manually installed it after the workshop agent's apply timed out), or a pending update proposal's target skill file has been removed, the proposal stays pending indefinitely and the Skill Workshop UI shows a misleading waiting state.

## Fix

Add detectStaleProposals() called from listSkillProposals() to detect such proposals and display them as stale without persisting — listSkillProposals stays read-only:
- create proposal: if the target skill file already exists → display as stale
- update proposal: if the target skill file has been removed → display as stale
- proposal record is never mutated from the read path; durable stale marking stays in lifecycle actions

Also extract markProposalStale() as a shared helper, replacing duplicate inline stale-marking at three existing call sites.

## Design note on read contract

This revision intentionally keeps listSkillProposals read-only. The stale status shown in the manifest is derived from the current workspace state at list time (same file-existence checks as before), but the proposal record is never mutated — no target lock, no writeMeta, no persist. Durable stale marking remains in lifecycle actions (apply / revise / reject). This resolves the operator.read write-surface concern: listing proposals is safe to call from any read-scoped client.

## Linked Issue

Closes #90388

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Skill Workshop proposal can remain shown as waiting in the UI even after the skill has already been installed manually and is visible/active in the skills list (#90388). A pending create proposal stays pending forever when its target skill already exists; similarly for pending update proposals when the target is deleted.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.22.0, tsx v4.22.3, Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.6.1 commit 1a3ce7c2a8. Branch fix/90388-skill-workshop-proposal-reconciliation on upstream/main.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Step 1 — standalone proof script exercising the actual workshop service code paths (listSkillProposals → detectStaleProposals):

node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-90421-workshop-reconciliation.mjs

(Script imports the real proposeCreateSkill, proposeUpdateSkill, listSkillProposals from src/skills/workshop/service.ts, sets up temp workspace/state dirs, creates pending proposals, simulates manual skill creation/deletion, and calls listSkillProposals() to exercise the detection path.)

Step 2 — vitest workshop service test suite:

OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts

**Evidence after fix:**

Before (no detection):

Create proposal stays pending forever after manual install:

$ node --import tsx/esm -e "
process.env.OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR='/tmp/proof-ws/state';
const {proposeCreateSkill, listSkillProposals} = await import('./src/skills/workshop/service.js');
const {writeSkill} = await import('./src/skills/test-support/e2e-test-helpers.js');
const ws = '/tmp/proof-ws/workspace';
await fs.mkdir(ws+'/skills',{recursive:true});
const p = await proposeCreateSkill({workspaceDir:ws, name:'Test', description:'d', content:'# T'});
await writeSkill({dir:ws+'/skills/test', name:'test', description:'d', body:'# T'});
const before = await listSkillProposals({workspaceDir:ws});
// WITHOUT fix: status stays 'pending' forever
console.log('status before fix:', before.proposals[0]?.status);
"
status before fix: pending

After (with fix — derived stale status, no record mutation):

$ node --import tsx/esm scripts/proof-90421-workshop-reconciliation.mjs
=== Real Behavior Proof: Workshop Proposal Reconciliation ===
Node.js: v22.22.0
State dir:  /tmp/proof-workshop-HnUDCC/state
Workspace:  /tmp/proof-workshop-HnUDCC/workspace

--- Scenario 1: Create proposal → manual install → reconciled stale ---

Step 1: Created proposal id=auto-helper-20260605-5010b4f3fa status=pending
Step 2: Before manual install — proposal status=pending
Step 3: Manually created target skill at .../skills/auto-helper/SKILL.md
Step 4: After listSkillProposals (with reconciliation) — proposal status=stale

PASS: Create proposal reconciled to stale when target skill already exists.

--- Scenario 2: Update proposal → target deleted → reconciled stale ---

Step 1: Created update proposal id=remove-me-20260605-55fe262a88 status=pending
Step 2: Deleted target skill file
Step 3: After listSkillProposals — proposal status=stale

PASS: Update proposal reconciled to stale when target skill is missing.

--- Scenario 3: No change → stays pending ---
Step 1: Created proposal id=keep-pending-20260605-df3c317360 status=pending
Step 2: After listSkillProposals — proposal status=pending

PASS: Proposal stays pending when no reconciliation is needed.

=== All scenarios complete (0 failure(s)) ===
exit code: 0

Vitest confirmation:

$ OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=4 pnpm test -- src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  24 passed (24)
   Start at  17:40:08
   Duration  6.47s
exit code: 0

**Observed result after fix:** The standalone proof script exercises the actual workshop service code (listSkillProposals → detectStaleProposals — read-only, no target lock, no persist). All 3 scenarios pass: create proposals reconciled when target skill manually installed, update proposals reconciled when target skill deleted, and proposals stay pending when no reconciliation needed. The 24 vitest tests all pass. The proof script exits nonzero on any failure scenario.

**What was not tested:** Live Skill Workshop UI in a running gateway. The derived stale status is computed at list time from workspace state, so UI polling or repeated listing is safe — no record mutations occur. A maintainer can use Mantis to capture visual proof: visual task: verify Skill Workshop shows a manually installed target proposal as stale instead of waiting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
